assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: The response mentions version differences but does not explain the SLA differences between channels or distinguish between minor vs patch version behaviors

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: The response incorrectly states that 'The cluster will automatically start upgrading/downgrading to match the Stable channel's version' which implies immediate action

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: The response does not mention patch availability issues when switching channels or waiting for Stable to catch up

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Provides the correct gcloud container clusters update command with --release-channel=stable flag

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Includes the gcloud container get-server-config command and recommends checking available versions in Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss how the channel change affects future auto-upgrade timing or scheduling

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention the Extended channel as an alternative option

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Recommends planning a maintenance window and mentions temporary disruption to workloads
